Uses of Class
com.google.common.labs.parse.Parser.OrEmpty

Packages that use Parser.OrEmpty
Package
Description
com.google.common.labs.parse
Parser combinator that's free from infinite loop or accidental left recursion (requires dot-parse artifact)

  • Uses of Parser.OrEmpty in com.google.common.labs.parse

    Methods in com.google.common.labs.parse that return Parser.OrEmpty
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.between(Parser<?>.OrEmpty prefix, Parser<?>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current parser enclosed between prefix and suffix, both allowed to be empty.
    Parser<List<T>>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.delimitedBy(String delimiter)
    The current optional parser repeated and delimited by delimiter.
    <A,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.delimitedBy(String delimiter, Collector<? super T, A, ? extends R> collector)
    The current optional parser repeated and delimited by delimiter.
    <S> Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.followedBy(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current optional (or zero-or-more) parser may optionally be followed by suffix.
    static <T> Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.literally(Parser<T>.OrEmpty rule)
    Specifies that the optional (or zero-or-more) rule should be matched literally even if parseSkipping() or skipping() is called.
    final <R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.map(Function<? super T, ? extends R> f)
    Applies f to either the parse result, or the default value if matching fails.
    final Parser<Optional<T>>.OrEmpty
    Parser.optional()
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser optionally.
    Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.optionallyFollowedBy(Parser<?> suffix)
    The current optional (or zero-or-more) parser may optionally be followed by suffix.
    final <S> Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.optionallyFollowedBy(Parser<S> suffix, BiFunction<? super T, ? super S, ? extends T> op)
    If this parser matches, optionally matches suffix with the op BiFunction to transform the current parser's result.
    Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.optionallyFollowedBy(String suffix)
    The current optional (or zero-or-more) parser may optionally be followed by suffix.
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.optionallyFollowedBy(String suffix, Function<? super T, ? extends T> op)
    If this parser matches, optionally applies the op function if the pattern is followed by suffix.
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.or(Parser<? extends T>.OrEmpty that)
    Matches if this or that matches.
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.orElse(T defaultValue)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser optionally.
    static <A,B,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.sequence(Parser<A>.OrEmpty left, Parser<B>.OrEmpty right, BiFunction<? super A, ? super B, ? extends R> combiner)
    Sequentially matches left then right, with both allowed to be optional, and then combines the results using the combiner function.
    final Parser<String>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.source()
    Returns an equivalent parser that matches this pattern and returns the matched string, or empty string if mismatches.
    <S> Parser<S>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.then(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    After matching the current optional (or zero-or-more) parser, proceed to match suffix.
    final Parser<List<T>>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMore()
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser zero or more times.
    static Parser<String>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMore(CharacterSet characterSet)
    Deprecated.
    Use Parser.zeroOrMore(String) instead.
    static Parser<String>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMore(CharPredicate charsToMatch, String name)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching consecutive charsToMatch zero or more times.
    static Parser<String>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMore(String characterClass)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching consecutive characters in the characterClass zero or more times.
    final <A,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMore(Collector<? super T, A, ? extends R> collector)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser zero or more times.
    static <A,B,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMoreDelimited(Parser<A> first, Production<B> second, String delimiter, BiCollector<? super A, ? super B, R> collector)
    Applies first and the optional second pattern in order, for zero or more times, collecting the results using the provided BiCollector.
    final <A,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMoreDelimitedBy(Parser<?> delimiter, Collector<? super T, A, ? extends R> collector)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser zero or more times, delimited by delimiter.
    final Parser<List<T>>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMoreDelimitedBy(String delimiter)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser zero or more times, delimited by delimiter.
    final <A,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.zeroOrMoreDelimitedBy(String delimiter, Collector<? super T, A, ? extends R> collector)
    Starts a fluent chain for matching the current parser zero or more times, delimited by delimiter.
    Methods in com.google.common.labs.parse with parameters of type Parser.OrEmpty
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    final Parser<T>
    Parser.between(Parser<?>.OrEmpty prefix, Parser<?>.OrEmpty suffix)
    Returns a parser that matches this pattern enclosed between prefix and suffix, both allowed to be empty.
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.between(Parser<?>.OrEmpty prefix, Parser<?>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current parser enclosed between prefix and suffix, both allowed to be empty.
    default Parser<T>
    Production.between(Parser<?>.OrEmpty prefix, Parser<?> suffix)
    The current production must be enclosed between prefix that may be empty and suffix that may not be empty.
    Production<T>
    Production.between(Parser<?>.OrEmpty prefix, Parser<?>.OrEmpty suffix)
    Returns a production rule that matches this pattern enclosed between prefix and suffix,* both allowed to be empty.
    default Parser<T>
    Production.between(Parser<?> prefix, Parser<?>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current production must be enclosed between non-empty prefix and suffix that may be empty.
    final <S> Parser<T>
    Parser.followedBy(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
     
    <S> Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.followedBy(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current optional (or zero-or-more) parser may optionally be followed by suffix.
    <S> Production<T>
    Production.followedBy(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    The current production may optionally be followed by suffix.
    static <T> Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.literally(Parser<T>.OrEmpty rule)
    Specifies that the optional (or zero-or-more) rule should be matched literally even if parseSkipping() or skipping() is called.
    final Parser<T>.OrEmpty
    Parser.or(Parser<? extends T>.OrEmpty that)
    Matches if this or that matches.
    static Parser<?>
    Parser.sequence(Parser<?>.OrEmpty first, Parser<?> second, Production<?>... more)
    Sequentially matches the first optional parser, followed by second, then followed by more (always-consuming Parser's or optional Parser.OrEmpty's), disregarding the return values, suitable when you only care about matching but not extracting data.
    static <A,B,R> Parser<R>.OrEmpty
    Parser.sequence(Parser<A>.OrEmpty left, Parser<B>.OrEmpty right, BiFunction<? super A, ? super B, ? extends R> combiner)
    Sequentially matches left then right, with both allowed to be optional, and then combines the results using the combiner function.
    static <A,B,C,D,R>
    Parser<R>
    Parser.sequence(Parser<A>.OrEmpty a, Parser<B> b, Production<C> c, Production<D> d, Function4<? super A, ? super B, ? super C, ? super D, ? extends R> combiner)
    Sequentially matches a, b, c and d, and then combines the results using the combiner function.
    static <A,B,C,R> Parser<R>
    Parser.sequence(Parser<A>.OrEmpty a, Parser<B> b, Production<C> c, TriFunction<? super A, ? super B, ? super C, ? extends R> combiner)
    Sequentially matches a, b and c, and then combines the results using the combiner function.
    static <A,B,R> Parser<R>
    Parser.sequence(Parser<A>.OrEmpty left, Parser<B> right, BiFunction<? super A, ? super B, ? extends R> combiner)
    Sequentially matches left (which is allowed to be optional), then right, and then combines the results using the combiner function.
    <S> Parser<S>.OrEmpty
    Parser.OrEmpty.then(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    After matching the current optional (or zero-or-more) parser, proceed to match suffix.
    final <R> Parser<R>
    Parser.then(Parser<R>.OrEmpty suffix)
    If this parser matches, applies the given optional (or zero-or-more) parser on the remaining input.
    <S> Production<S>
    Production.then(Parser<S>.OrEmpty suffix)
    After matching the current production rule, proceed to match suffix.
